column1: Self-sacrifice for the salvation or protection of others: Seen in Jesus (crucifixion), Chenrezig (vow to save all beings), Jizo (remaining to help souls), Amida Buddha (vow to save all who call his name), and Maitreya (future savior).
Death and rebirth/transformation: Dionysus (dismemberment and return), Shiva (dance of destruction and creation), Mithras (bull-slaying as cosmic renewal), Buddha (enlightenment after renunciation).
Compassion and loving-kindness: Krishna (divine play and protection), Chenrezig, Jizo, Amida Buddha, Jesus, Maitreya.
Mediator or savior roles: Mithras (mediator between humanity and Ahura Mazda), Jesus (mediator between humanity and God), Chenrezig and Jizo (intercessors for the suffering).
Promise of future salvation or hope: Maitreya (future Buddha), Jesus (second coming), Amida Buddha (Pure Land), Jizo (helping until Maitreya’s arrival).

These themes and parables reflect a shared mythic structure across world traditions: the divine figure who sacrifices, mediates, or returns to save, and whose compassion or transformative power offers hope and renewal to humanity1236789.
